customer_id.business_reasoning: Customer ID is the unique primary identifier. Rows with missing customer ID are orphaned and cannot be associated with any entity.
customer_id.risk_assessment: Low. Essential for relational integrity and accurate analytics.

email.business_reasoning: Email address is critical for communications and identity resolution. Operating on rows with missing email poses compliance and communication risks.
email.risk_assessment: Low. Dropping ensures high-quality communications, but slightly reduces dataset size.

amount.business_reasoning: Using the median transaction amount maintains distribution consistency and minimizes skew from extreme transaction outliers.
amount.risk_assessment: Low. Prevents skewing the financial mean, but might reduce overall variance.

quantity.value_used: N/A (Column not present in current dataset)
quantity.business_reasoning: Quantity must be a positive integer. Imputing with the median prevents fractional quantities and skewed counts.
quantity.risk_assessment: Low. Safe default for transactional statistics.

category.business_reasoning: Mode imputation fills missing categorizations with the most common category, preserving qualitative data distributions.
category.risk_assessment: Low. Safe assuming missingness is low, but can over-represent the modal category.

region.value_used: N/A (Column not present in current dataset)
region.business_reasoning: Assigns the most common geographic region to balance territorial marketing segmentation.
region.risk_assessment: Low. May introduce minor localized bias if region distribution is highly uniform.

status_date.value_used: N/A (Column not present in current dataset)
status_date.business_reasoning: Time-based status updates assume a record retains its state until the next chronologically recorded change.
status_date.risk_assessment: Medium. Assumes no changes occurred between log times, which might introduce temporal staleness.

last_updated.value_used: N/A (Column not present in current dataset)
last_updated.business_reasoning: Timestamps of last update in sequential logs are forward-filled under the assumption of state persistence.
last_updated.risk_assessment: Medium. Might delay the true status transition time, but preserves correct sequence ordering.
